[2018-01-12 20:59] resonance22: do either of you think that a large influx of players would help and discourage smurfing?
[2018-01-12 21:00] resonance22: i want to have a large discussion about this later this month to make sure we get it right. and i am always open to revisiting topics. if a smurf policy isnt working, we'll revise it ;)
[2018-01-12 21:01] crash_overlord: I actually don't think specific smurfs are bad at all (OTP smurfs in League for example, or what Arka wants to do) and regular smurfs for really high ladder players could be arranged in a sense that they start at rank 9 or 10 as they're created since they're usually used just to decrease queue times.
[2018-01-12 21:02] elyot: The policy thus far has been "having multiple accounts is against the ToS, but we generally only ban smurfs if they're causing trouble"
[2018-01-12 21:02] crash_overlord: The biggest problem of smurfs from what I know is that very new players/"worse" players feel really bad when they dont think they can do ANYTHING to win
[2018-01-12 21:03] elyot: new players generally cannot tell the difference between losing to somebody decent vs losing to a top-tier player
[2018-01-12 21:03] crash_overlord: Is that prismata specific?
[2018-01-12 21:04] resonance22: totally agreed, so perhaps when new players sign up they can tie an account together and input maybe an existing email and password to start at a higher rank as crash sort of suggested. it's something to keep an eye on at least because launch day is SUPER IMPORTANT that all new players get a good first game
[2018-01-12 21:04] elyot: It's not like a game like league/SC or a fighting game where you just get destroyed, the experience is not nearly as negative and is not perceptable at all. Plus our automatcher is VERY good at matching people high if they win a lot as new accounts
[2018-01-12 21:05] resonance22: but at least we have an excellent campaign so retention should be higher even with smurfs if we get the new players to enjoy the campaign first
[2018-01-12 21:06] resonance22: i do kind of want to add an option when creating an account idk, if you have an existing account to link to, to start a higher tier to avoid smurfing as an issue entirely. 🤔 food for thought
[2018-01-12 21:07] resonance22: it also prevents problems with us not internally knowing who is who, the linked accounts could not be public info

[2018-01-12 21:07] iminabearsuit: It's kind of useful, if people want to plan ahead.
[2018-01-12 21:08] resonance22: i will ask around but the answer is yes but i dont know when. we're working like all day and all night to get this out in late January ideally but we cant give a specific date yet because a bug could come up at the last second
[2018-01-12 21:08] resonance22: and then you lose a lot of community trust
[2018-01-12 21:08] resonance22: we're VERY CLOSE though i assure you
[2018-01-12 21:08] resonance22: as a youtuber i need to know in advance, and many of you stream too

messages.content: i agree that the best way is to disincentivize them. i also agree with the problem of the feeling of rating being stolen

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: do either of you think that a large influx of players would help and discourage smurfing?

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: i want to have a large discussion about this later this month to make sure we get it right. and i am always open to revisiting topics. if a smurf policy isnt working, we'll revise it ;)

messages.author: crash_overlord
messages.content: I actually don't think specific smurfs are bad at all (OTP smurfs in League for example, or what Arka wants to do) and regular smurfs for really high ladder players could be arranged in a sense that they start at rank 9 or 10 as they're created since they're usually used just to decrease queue times.

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: The policy thus far has been "having multiple accounts is against the ToS, but we generally only ban smurfs if they're causing trouble"

messages.author: crash_overlord
messages.content: The biggest problem of smurfs from what I know is that very new players/"worse" players feel really bad when they dont think they can do ANYTHING to win

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: new players generally cannot tell the difference between losing to somebody decent vs losing to a top-tier player

messages.author: crash_overlord
messages.content: Is that prismata specific?

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: totally agreed, so perhaps when new players sign up they can tie an account together and input maybe an existing email and password to start at a higher rank as crash sort of suggested. it's something to keep an eye on at least because launch day is SUPER IMPORTANT that all new players get a good first game

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: It's not like a game like league/SC or a fighting game where you just get destroyed, the experience is not nearly as negative and is not perceptable at all. Plus our automatcher is VERY good at matching people high if they win a lot as new accounts

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: but at least we have an excellent campaign so retention should be higher even with smurfs if we get the new players to enjoy the campaign first

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: i do kind of want to add an option when creating an account idk, if you have an existing account to link to, to start a higher tier to avoid smurfing as an issue entirely. 🤔 food for thought

messages.author: resonance22
messages.content: it also prevents problems with us not internally knowing who is who, the linked accounts could not be public info
